purpose: Patch the global edge parser by adding indexed 30-entry carrier-law table witnesses.
boundary: This does not close Gap A.
This patches the witness parser, not the theorem.
Indexed global witnesses show that a column is represented in a 30-entry global law table.
Indexed witnesses are weaker than direct endpoint agreement.

claim.short_form: The parser patch adds indexed 30-entry global law-table witnesses, separating representation by index from direct endpoint agreement.
claim.not_closed_reason: Indexed carrier witnesses are still not a full global source-law predicate evaluation.

interpretation.main_result: The missing direct edge hits are covered by indexed global law-table witnesses if the 30-entry tables are accepted as column-indexed carrier witnesses.
interpretation.important_caution: Direct endpoint agreement remains weaker than indexed coverage; this is a parser bridge, not a proof closure.
interpretation.next_move: Rerun the global carrier map using direct-or-indexed witness modes and then evaluate whether the normalized source law can be stated as a global indexed-carrier predicate.

checks.detail: parser patch gives indexed witnesses, not global predicate proof
